item.title: jesus is a montagne of cheese
item.content:encoded:
Following S.Dali hypothesis on jesus : "jesus is a montagne of cheese" coming from psalm 68:16-17 of st augustin which have many translations one being that god (jesus) is a montagne of fat and milk (septante version and vulgate version both being ones of the oldest version of the catholic bible). Following this I believe that horace "big cheese" is a manifestation of jesus in the fith religion making it the second coming of jesus.
you can look this up it's true

item.title: Question about Image Licensing
item.content:encoded:
Update: Elogee FishTruck PM'd and assured me that this image is already in the public domain. Thank you very much!

Well, while I'm so happy that 'Big Cheese' Horace came back to the wiki once again (and even got his own tag!), I still would like the staff to clarify about the license of this image.

According to National Geographic, the original image was taken by Thomas Riggs Jr. — Circa 1900.
https://www.nationalgeographic.com/science/photos/smallpox/
"After a long day treating smallpox-infected Indians in the Yukon Territory, circa 1900, a doctor fumigates all but his head inside a formaldehyde tent. After about ten minutes the germs were considered dead."

Is this image considered as in public domain or similar condition? Or National Geographic has its legitimate copyright? If the latter is in case, regrettably, I really don't think this image is available on this wiki.

If necessary, I'd like to provide substitute images, which are CC-compliant. It may not "A man's head sticking out of tent" image, but "A man holding a smoking pipe in mouth" image could be found.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hold on, Auguest 23rd is the first day of Virgo(As far as I know).. Is it intentional and/or relevant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this classic absurdist shit. I think it's MUCH more difficult to pull off well, like this, than most would think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly, I am appalled that this is not at +300 yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still love this, but very much not a fan of the "related groups of interest" dealy at the bottom. It's redundant with the tag and just jars the reader out of the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People getting turned into weed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I finally did it. Better late than never, right?

item.title: Re: Funny, I don't recall this.
item.content:encoded: Thats a damn shame. When building scps are real buildings it gives me chills and makes the article so much better.

item.title: Funny, I don't recall this.
item.content:encoded: I lived in Blairsville, GA for almost a decade and I don't recall seeing this building at all. ;)

item.title: Happy +100
item.content:encoded: I'm not quite sure whether I want to give this an upvote myself (part of its regional charm gets lost for non-USians), but it's at the very least an unexpected spin on the Fifthist Church.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: from the south here. we have waste bins in the Florida room and out on the lanai.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hell, I liked it, was ready to toss a vote in for the parrot and squirrel logs alone.

Just to be a contrarian cockbite here, I didn't find it spooky or scary, just funny in an amusingly weird way, even if they do burn people alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Granted, no one else in the South curses in church (beyond the damn).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This continues to be great. One more tiny thing, though:

waste bin

I'm assuming that the guy is supposed to be from the South, no? I have never heard anyone in the south say "waste bin." "Trash can" for paper, "garbage can" for not-paper. But that might just be me.

item.title: Suggestions from a nervous newbie
item.content:encoded:
I really like this (especially the radio broadcasts), but there are some structure/tone problems that detract from the overall quality of the piece. As a relative newbie, I'm just not sure how much editing I can get away with, so here are my suggestions:

SCP-1982's notable effects at most times only present themselves with the appearance of SCP-1982-1, if SCP-1982-1 appears on 08/23.

This is a really confusing sentence.

SCP-1982-1 have been limited to residents of Georgia and outlying states noted to have significant wealth and fame.

This sounds like the outlying states are wealthy and famous. How about "limited to wealthy, famous [or notable] residents of Georgia…"

Singing vocalizations from multiple entities appear to be coming from a pipe in the entities mouth

The pipe is in the man's mouth, so I think this should be "entity's".

Lastly, "SCP-1982-1 does not appear to have any compulsion to travel to or enter the vicinity of SCP-1982." How do they get there, then?

Sorry for the nitpicks on a really interesting SCP. I'm still not comfortable editing other people's articles (other than for obvious spelling errors), but I'm trying to learn and contribute a little more every day. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found myself laughing heavily halfway through reading whilst uttering the phrase "What the bloody heck is this thing…" I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, I don't like it. Looks like someone made a Fifth Church article just to make a Fifth Church article. Then again, I'm not a huge fan of the church anyways. All I get from it is a Scientology knockoff.

Either way, this article does nothing for me but convolute an already convoluted concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my new favorite thing on this site, and if "Roll that frog footage!" becomes enough of a meme for J.K. Simmons to read it out with the same passion that he once decried lemons, then I will die happy, become reincarnated and die happy again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And as my recording time is severely limited, I didn't get to do it this week. Watch the skies!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got that mental image, too, until I saw the second picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deliciously weird. I really want to read more Fifthist stuff. Maybe a tale…? Seriously, its like Captain Beefheart writing the scripture for Scientology.

That said…

one would need to be adjacent to SCP-1982 to observe it

This sounds so off it nearly canceled my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The smoke thing could be taken as part of a larger credo. Big Cheese makes a big deal of leaving the body behind, which I think is their thing. So in what other ways could this be accomplished? Why do they choose who they choose? Why do they keep them around afterward? Consider the church's goals and beliefs and you'll find your expansion angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to say "fuckin' cluckatoo".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed. In fact, I'd go farther, and say it'd be better to extinguish this now and let people rem-ember it fondly than to burn everybody out on the idea like AWCY did.

(Puns totally intended, but I mean this sincerely, too.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My honest suggestion, if you pardon the pun, is to let the idea smolder for a bit. Let there be some time between this and the next Fifthism SCP.

item.title: Re: Catchphrase!
item.content:encoded: Before the intermission, a title card will appear informing the audience that y'all can go take a quick piss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering it may (or may not) broadcast sensitive information, I would say yeah.
Considering how easy it is to scramble radio transmissions, I don't see why they would not cover that front.
I'm being overly-analytic, it's still up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure thing, I could write more dialogue too if you want. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lumancer, that's not the only danger. It also runs the risk of being a Scientology parody. I'm going to try to expand in the next SCP. Any suggestions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah but it's a Foundation for containing anomalous items, not scary ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little leery that fifthism is going to get stuck in a rut of people-as-scented-smoke without other interesting content, but that aside, this is solidly weird. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't just let anyone in though. They've relented a few times for experimental purposes but they never could catch what goes on when someone goes in. The whole last series of quotes is about how the church has been lingering for years without people coming in, so desperate that they are taking in parrots and squirrels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1 is the person being let in.

Why the deuce is the Foundation letting poor sods wander in to an SCP? The Foundation would be grossly negligent for letting anyone get far enough for them to document the effects, and I'd only see themselves tossing a D-class or two in for experimenting, not allowing civilians through.

Also, how the heck are only two guards supposed to secure an area two kilometers in diameter? Seriously?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't just a good use of the Fifth Church, it's one that's inspired me with regard to what the group is and does.

I approve and have voted accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH. That's why he was… Dammit, I parsed that as him having some kind of speaking pipe emerging from his mouth. Like, a metal pipe. Now everything makes sense.

But why isn't he the -1? He's part of the SCP, an entity that only appears under certain circumstances, and that's usually what gets the designation.

I have designs on doing a reading of the broadcast excerpts over the weekend, but that will depend on A) whether I can do a decent voice for it, and B) whether or not someone else beats me to the punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is my favorite kind of upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't even…
What did I just…

This article is crazy, make that Crazy. L. Ron Hubbard meets George Clinton via Southern Baptist.

I upvoted, but I feel bad about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see now. Somehow, I didn't see the part about turning people into smells at first. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is just my personal preference. While some non-scary SCPs like the tear-shaped dudes with one eye that are in the top 50 and could have been used as a watching mechanism for 173 (sorry I can't remember the number) are great, there is something both attractive and disturbing in the right scary scp. I don't say this is bad and I see the scary content (Extradimensional church, with a weird dude, and a random radio chatter is GREAT), I still feel a bit overwhelmed by the funny part. And that is, for me, a 50:50.

item.title: Re: Catchphrase!
item.content:encoded: Personally, I cannot wait for a chance to work "Fuckin cluckatoo twoshoes the no money-havin' fuckin' dancin' parrot!" into a conversation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really hope that I wasn't the only person imagining the preacher as professional wrestler Dusty Rhodes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The footnote there says "with the exception of one parrot, and one squirrel"

E.g. specific humanoid with those 2 exceptions.

Unless you're trying to imply in an awkward manner that "exception" should be plural?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, format fixed.

I don't think a group of wackos broadcasting in 2km in a sparsely populated town once a year is cause for alarm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Desc. Par. 3;

When a specific humanoid 1…

1. With the exception of one parrot (August, 23, 1998), and one squirrel 08/23/2002.

-Advise you look up the definition of humanoid. One could stretch it for a squirrel but certainly not a bird. Or am I missing something here? humanoid
-Also, using two different date formats in a single sentence? No matter the format you use through a work, try to be consistent.

Also, I don't know if I'm the only one but I had the re-read many passages to figure out exactly what was going on. Even now, I'm not sure I got it all right.
I'm not sure about the radio transmissions though. Isn't that a pretty big containment breach risk? Shouldn't there be countermeasures in place to stop anyone from being able to hear any of that?

I still liked the article overall but I'm withholding my vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know how any of you pinks would have known about that…

(yes it was a direct reference, praise Bob)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I'm always surprised at what people think is scary. Not trying to be funny, but these pictures/articles are always bland to me at the time of posting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Celebration "Big Cheese" Horace reminds me of Bob Dobbs, only more psychotic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reverend is not designated by a number because he does not have much dialogue or action in the logs. He only makes a brief appearance.

-1 is the person being let in. -A is the congregation as pipe tobacco.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Singing vocalizations from multiple entities appear to be coming from a pipe in the entities mouth (referred to in this log as SCP-1982-A). The entity itself does not speak, and makes abnormal facial expressions throughout the course of the interview.

I read that, and thought of something along the lines of a Noise Marine - dude with like a three inch pipe coming out of his mouth, and making tortured faces because of it.

I like the picture, but it was a bit of a shock…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the crap.

+1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird as hell. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 1998 transcript is probably the best thing I've read all day. I had concerns that this was similar to the anomalous religions broadcasts article done recently, but then I figured out what the church was.

My only question then is that it seemed very vague from the wording in the article whether -1 is the Reverend or the person/being entering the church. In fact, by the end, I actually have no idea whatsoever.

item.title: Catchphrase!
item.content:encoded: Mark my words: when the SCPF becomes a major Hollywood blockbuster, everybody and their grandmother will be going around saying "Roll that frog footage, Sandy!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good horror. The kind that doesn't need to show you entrails hanging from the walls. The kind that your mind instinctively recoils from, because it knows only that it is Wrong. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… I'm going to upvote. Print this out. And burn it tonight. I'm now insane. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What Trinitite said.

This church is to other churches what clowns are to people. There are some superficial similarities but all the wrong things are being emphasized and exaggerated so you can't quite put your finger on what is wrong and it's kind of hysterical but get surrounded by it and it is pure nightmare fuel. Sure it isn't out to get you but what does it mean? Why is it there? What if it called to you?

For me the best part is that something weird and outside human understanding is going on and it happens to be in the guise of a church for arbitrary reasons as opposed to specific ones. The way that this doesn't read like it is trying to be a parody of a church makes it all the weirder and creepier.

Also, the Foundation is keeping it from getting what it wants and it's merely grumpy about it instead of magically killing everyone in retaliation. But they still can't figure it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, this is plenty scary for me. Look at it this way: it's a 'church' (Scientology-style) that's about turning people into smells and burning incense, that seems to make smoking…stuff out of squirrels and parrots, and it can take a soul out of the body. The fact that they're jolly about that is even worse.
